title: cynanchum adalinae subsp. mannii
intro: Cynanchum adalinae subsp. mannii, also known as Mann's swallowwort or Adalina's swallowwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Ethiopian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits associated with Cynanchum adalinae subsp. mannii include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential use in wound healing and as an anti-aging agent. Herbal preparations of Cynanchum adalinae subsp. mannii typically involve the use of the plant's roots, leaves, and flowers, which can be consumed as teas, infusions, or decoctions.
